location.origin: Pacific Northwest. Chris Pureka is a queer folk singer-songwriter based in the PNW. Duwamish ancestral territory. Part of the region's vibrant folk and queer arts community.
location.formative: Pacific Northwest. Pureka's formative geography is the PNW folk scene and queer arts community -- house concerts, small venues, KEXP sessions, Bandcamp, the queer folk circuit. The self-released model reflects both artistic independence and community infrastructure.
location.career: Chris Pureka: Driving North (2006, self-released -- debut, raw acoustic folk, PNW landscapes). How I Got Here (2010, self-released -- more polished, autobiographical). Back in the Ring (2016, self-released -- return after hiatus, fuller arrangements). Three albums across ten years. Self-released throughout. No label. Rich androgynous voice. Deeply personal lyrics about identity, love, and loss. Nonbinary/genderqueer. First openly queer/nonbinary artist in the engine.

labels: Self-released (all albums -- Driving North 2006, How I Got Here 2010, Back in the Ring 2016; no label infrastructure; complete creative control; the purest form of independence in the engine; reflects both artistic choice and the self-sustaining queer folk circuit)

members.name: Chris Pureka
members.note: Solo artist. Nonbinary/genderqueer. Rich, androgynous voice -- a register that resists gendered categorization. Deeply personal, introspective lyrics about identity, love, and loss. The voice IS the instrument. First openly queer/nonbinary artist in the engine.

associated_musicians.name: Mirah
associated_musicians.note: The engine's queer thread: Mirah at degree 50 (Indie Folk, Olympia, K Records) and Pureka at degree 76 (Folk, PNW, self-released). Twenty-six degrees apart. Different cities, different labels, same identity thread. Both queer artists whose identity is artistically central.

associated_musicians.name: Jesse Sykes
associated_musicians.note: Both non-male folk artists operating outside the mainstream. Sykes's folk is dark and atmospheric. Pureka's folk is confessional and identity-based. Different registers of outsider perspective in folk.

associated_musicians.name: Briana Marela
associated_musicians.note: Both non-male. Both push boundaries -- Marela through technology (vocal looping), Pureka through identity (androgynous register). The layered loop (72) versus the androgynous register (76). Technology versus authenticity.

associated_musicians.name: Shelby Earl
associated_musicians.note: Both E=2 folk artists. Both quiet and intimate. Earl's intimacy is romantic and domestic. Pureka's intimacy is identity-based and political. Same energy level, different vulnerability.

associated_musicians.name: Kris Orlowski
associated_musicians.note: Both value directness and emotional honesty. Both operate at smaller scales. Orlowski's earnestness is relational. Pureka's earnestness is existential.

theory_nodes.concept: The androgynous register (seventy-seventh acoustic element -- the acoustic quality of a voice that resists gendered categorization, a register that is neither conventionally male nor conventionally female but occupies the space between and beyond those categories; Pureka's rich, deep, resonant, unadorned voice embodies the gender identity of the artist; the first acoustic element defined by gender-acoustic identity rather than genre-acoustic character)
examples: Chris Pureka (degree 76) -- rich, deep, androgynous voice that resists gendered categorization. The voice is not 'unusual' -- it is accurate. It sounds like the person singing. The androgynous register is not an affectation but an expression of nonbinary identity.

Contrast with the ache (degree 58, Death Cab for Cutie) -- emotional precision under indie rock. The ache is gendered (Gibbard's male voice yearning). The androgynous register is ungendered voice existing.
Contrast with the manuscript page (degree 57, Laura Gibson) -- literary precision in folk form. A female voice writing. The androgynous register is a voice that refuses the category.
Contrast with the murmur (degree 45, Damien Jurado) -- whispered intimacy, a male voice. The androgynous register is a voice that does not declare its gender.
Contrast with the layered loop (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- technology multiplying a female voice. The androgynous register is a single unprocessed voice.
Contrast with the communal harmony (degree 53, Fleet Foxes) -- many voices dissolving into collective sound. The androgynous register is one voice dissolving the gender binary.
The androgynous register opens a new dimension: not just what the voice sounds like but what the voice IS in terms of identity.
theory_nodes.description: The androgynous register is the seventy-seventh acoustic element in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the acoustic quality of a voice that resists gendered categorization -- rich, deep, resonant, unadorned, occupying the space between and beyond conventional gender categories. In Chris Pureka's music, the voice itself embodies nonbinary identity. The first acoustic element defined by gender-acoustic identity.

ancestors: The manuscript page (degree 57, Laura Gibson) -- literary precision in folk form. The androgynous register is identity precision in folk form.
The layered loop (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- technology multiplying voice. The androgynous register is unprocessed voice refusing categorization.
The murmur (degree 45, Damien Jurado) -- whispered male voice. The androgynous register is a voice without gender declaration.
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-seven acoustic elements across seventy-seven degrees. The androgynous register follows the reverb wash (74) -- from atmospheric dispersion to identity expression. Track element 78 at degree 77.

theory_nodes.concept: The identity artist (seventy-seventh artist-city pattern -- artists whose personal identity is the primary mode of authenticity in their artistic practice; the personal is not just political but aesthetic; identity IS the art; Chris Pureka's queerness and nonbinary identity are the substance of the music, not biographical footnotes)
examples: Chris Pureka (degree 76) -- queerness and nonbinary identity are artistically central. The lyrics explore identity. The voice embodies identity. The career operates within identity-based community infrastructure (queer folk circuit). Remove the identity and the art loses its center.
Contrast with the side project (degree 74, Poor Moon) -- defined by band membership. The identity artist is defined by selfhood.
Contrast with the international student (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- defined by geographic movement. The identity artist is defined by internal identity.
Contrast with the departed member (degree 67, Chris Walla) -- defined by band relationship. The identity artist is defined by self-relationship.
Contrast with the writer-musician (degree 57, Laura Gibson) -- defined by dual discipline. The identity artist is defined by who they are.
The identity artist opens the engine to identity as an aesthetic category, not just a demographic one.
theory_nodes.description: The identity artist is the seventy-seventh artist-city pattern in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes artists whose personal identity -- gender, sexuality, or other dimensions of selfhood -- is the primary mode of authenticity in their artistic practice. Chris Pureka is the engine's first identity artist. Queerness and nonbinary identity are the substance of the music.

ancestors: The side project (degree 74, Poor Moon) -- band membership as identity. The identity artist is selfhood as identity.
The international student (degree 72, Briana Marela) -- geographic identity. The identity artist is personal identity.
The writer-musician (degree 57, Laura Gibson) -- disciplinary identity. The identity artist is existential identity.
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-seven artist-city patterns across seventy-seven degrees. The identity artist follows the side project -- from collective to individual identity. Track pattern 78 at degree 77.

theory_nodes.concept: Blue note -- the coming-out (seventy-sixth deployment; the blue note that sounds when an artist's public identity does not match the world's assumptions; the gap between how the world categorizes you and who you actually are; the folk genre's assumed heteronormativity versus Pureka's queer reality; the listener's uncertainty about the voice's gender sustains the blue note)
examples: Chris Pureka (degree 76) -- the blue note plays in the space between the folk tradition's assumed heteronormativity and Pureka's queer reality. The folk genre has a history of outsider voices, but queerness in folk has often been coded rather than explicit. Pureka's blue note is the sound of explicitness.
The blue note also plays in the androgynous register: the listener hears the voice and cannot categorize it. The uncertainty is the blue note. The voice resists resolution into 'male' or 'female.'
Contrast with the reverb trail (degree 74, Poor Moon) -- spatial dissolution. The coming-out is identity dissolution between assumed and actual self.
Contrast with the thread (degree 70, Horse Feathers) -- two instruments approaching the same pitch. The coming-out is one identity approaching recognition.
Contrast with the indie-to-major crossing (degree 58, Death Cab) -- audience split at label transition. The coming-out is audience split at identity revelation.
theory_nodes.description: The coming-out is the seventy-sixth blue note deployment in the Seattle 360 engine. It describes the blue note that sounds when an artist's public identity does not match the world's assumptions -- the gap between categorization and reality. In the androgynous register, the listener's uncertainty sustains the blue note.

ancestors: Blue note -- the reverb trail (degree 74, Poor Moon): spatial dissolution. The coming-out is identity dissolution.
Blue note -- the thread (degree 70, Horse Feathers): two sources approaching. The coming-out is one identity approaching recognition.
All seventy-five prior blue note deployments (degrees 0-75)
genealogy.relationship: Seventy-six deployments across seventy-seven degrees. Track deployment 77 at degree 77.

theory_nodes.concept: Folk at Genre Stage 58 -- confessional folk; the genre's most personal register; direct, first-person, emotionally exposed storytelling; voice-and-guitar tradition; identity-based lyrics; distinct from literary folk (57), dark folk (64), chamber folk (70)
examples: Chris Pureka (degree 76) -- Folk. Genre Stage 58. Confessional folk.
Folk returns after the genre has been the engine's most-visited family. The confessional register is distinct: direct, first-person, identity-based.
Contrast with Laura Gibson (degree 57) -- literary folk. Gibson's precision is literary. Pureka's precision is personal.
Contrast with Jesse Sykes (degree 64) -- dark atmospheric folk. Sykes's folk is atmospheric and cinematic. Pureka's folk is intimate and confessional.
Contrast with Horse Feathers (degree 70) -- chamber folk. Horse Feathers' folk is ensemble-based. Pureka's folk is solo voice-and-guitar.
theory_nodes.description: Folk at degree 76 establishes Genre Stage 58. Confessional folk -- the genre's most personal register. Direct, first-person, identity-based storytelling. Voice-and-guitar tradition. Distinct from literary folk (57), dark folk (64), and chamber folk (70).

ancestors: Folk at degree 57 (Laura Gibson) -- literary folk. Pureka's confessional folk is more personal, less literary.
Indie Folk/Rock at degree 74 (Poor Moon) -- hybrid genre. Pureka's pure folk strips away the rock element.
genealogy.relationship: Genre Stage 58 at degree 76. Folk returns. Confessional register. Track genre at degree 77.

theory_nodes.concept: E=2 at degree 76 -- twenty-seventh E=2 deployment; distribution 3:27:19:18:10:2; self-released as inherent E=2 calibration; quiet register; house concerts, small venues, queer folk circuit
examples: Chris Pureka (degree 76) -- E=2. Self-released. Quiet register. No label infrastructure.
E=2 rises to 27 deployments. Distribution: E1:3, E2:27, E3:19, E4:18, E5:10, E6:2. Total: 77.
E=2 extends its lead as the engine's dominant energy level.
Self-released is inherent E=2: no marketing budget, no distribution network beyond digital. The energy reflects operational scale.
E=2 at degree 76 returns the engine to its quiet register after a recent mix of middle and higher energies.
theory_nodes.description: E=2 at degree 76 is the twenty-seventh E=2 deployment in the Seattle 360 engine. Energy distribution: E1:3, E2:27, E3:19, E4:18, E5:10, E6:2. Self-released as inherent E=2 calibration. The quiet register returns.

ancestors: E=2 at degree 55 (Shelby Earl): quiet folk. The quiet folk quadrant extends.
E=2 at prior deployments. 27th E=2. Dominant energy level.
genealogy.relationship: Twenty-seven E=2 deployments across seventy-seven degrees. Distribution 3:27:19:18:10:2. Track at degree 77.

theory_nodes.concept: Gender identity milestone at degree 76 -- first openly queer/nonbinary artist; binary framework limitation acknowledged; queer thread Mirah (50) + Pureka (76); gender ratio 22:55 (~28.6%)
examples: Chris Pureka (degree 76) -- first openly queer/nonbinary artist. Gender identity milestone.
The engine's gender tracking used binary (non-male vs. male). Pureka reveals the limitation. The engine acknowledges and adapts.
Queer thread: Mirah (50) and Pureka (76). Twenty-six degrees apart. Different cities, labels, same identity thread.
Gender ratio (counting Pureka as non-male): 22:55 (~28.6%). 30% threshold approaching.
The milestone opens identity as an analytical dimension beyond demographic counting.
theory_nodes.description: The gender identity milestone at degree 76 marks the engine's first openly queer/nonbinary artist. The binary framework limitation is acknowledged. The queer thread connects Mirah (50) and Pureka (76). Gender ratio: 22:55 (~28.6%). Identity becomes an aesthetic category.

ancestors: Gender tracking from degree 0 onward -- binary framework. Degree 76 reveals its limitation.
Mirah at degree 50 -- first queer artist connection. Degree 76 makes the thread explicit.
genealogy.relationship: Gender identity milestone at degree 76. First queer/nonbinary artist. Binary limitation acknowledged. Queer thread: 2 entries. Track at degree 77.

theory_nodes.concept: Territorial acknowledgment: Duwamish territory at degree 76 -- PNW-based artist; Duwamish territory continuous; seven territories mapped
examples: Degree 76 -- Duwamish territory (Seattle/PNW). Pureka is PNW-based. The Duwamish territorial acknowledgment continues.
Seven Coast Salish territories remain mapped.
theory_nodes.description: The territorial acknowledgment at degree 76 is Duwamish territory -- Pureka is PNW-based. Seven Coast Salish territories remain mapped.

ancestors: Duwamish territory at multiple prior Seattle/PNW degrees.
genealogy.relationship: Duwamish territory at degree 76. Seven territories mapped. Track at degree 77.

cross_references.relationship: Dark folk, women in folk. Both non-male folk artists operating outside the mainstream. Sykes's folk is dark and atmospheric. Pureka's folk is confessional and identity-based. Different registers of outsider perspective.
Non-male, experimental. Both push boundaries -- Marela through technology (vocal looping), Pureka through identity (androgynous register). The layered loop (72) versus the androgynous register (76).
Quiet folk women. Both E=2 folk artists. Both quiet and intimate. Earl's intimacy is romantic. Pureka's intimacy is identity-based. Same energy, different vulnerability.
Earnest, personal. Both value directness and emotional honesty at smaller scales. Orlowski's earnestness is relational. Pureka's earnestness is existential.
Queer artist connection. The engine's queer thread: Mirah (50, Olympia, K Records) and Pureka (76, PNW, self-released). Twenty-six degrees apart. Different cities, labels, same identity thread.
